message:
feat(storage,cli,share): canonical-path archive locking, startup check hardening, and share executor resilience

Storage (lib.rs):
- archive_process_lock now keys on canonical filesystem path (PathBuf)
  instead of project slug string. This prevents two archives that resolve
  to the same directory via different slug derivations from acquiring
  separate locks, which could cause data corruption under concurrent
  writes.
- Add normalize_repo_root_key() to canonicalize repo roots in both
  CommitQueue and CommitCoalescer, ensuring consistent deduplication
  regardless of whether the caller provides a symlinked or real path.
- open_archive: detect symlinks in the project root path hierarchy and
  refuse to open (prevents confusion between symlinked and real dirs).
  Also use symlink_metadata instead of is_dir to avoid following symlinks.
- Signal debounce keys now include the notifications signals directory
  path, preventing cross-config collisions when multiple server instances
  share state.

CLI (lib.rs):
- Expanded CLI functionality for archive management operations.

DB (pool.rs, reconstruct.rs):
- Connection pool hardening and reconstruction improvements.

Server (startup_checks.rs):
- Expanded startup validation including archive integrity checks.

Share (bundle.rs, deploy.rs, executor.rs, finalize.rs, prompt.rs,
       snapshot.rs, wizard.rs):
- Executor resilience improvements for share publishing workflow.
- Bundle and finalization hardening for partial-publish cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
